notes:
ghê = scary, frightening. It describes something that makes you feel fear or shock.

It’s an adjective and usually comes after the noun: phim ghê, cảnh ghê. It can also go after verbs to comment on a situation: đau ghê, lạnh ghê (very painful / very cold).

In Southern speech, ghê often just means “very” or “intensely,” not truly scary. Close in meaning to đáng sợ (more formal, clearly “scary”).
exampleSentences: Bộ phim tối qua anh xem thật sự rất ghê, em không dám coi lại.
Chị nhìn thấy con nhện to ghê trong phòng tắm nên hét lên.
Trời mưa to và gió mạnh làm cho không khí ngoài đường tối nay ghê quá.

definitions.es: Se utiliza para describir algo que es aterrador o repugnante.
definitions.en: Used to describe something that is frightening or disgusting.
definitions.vi: Dùng để mô tả điều gì đó đáng sợ hoặc ghê tởm.

exampleSentencesTranslated: The movie I watched last night was really scary, I don't dare to watch it again.
She saw a scary big spider in the bathroom and screamed.
Heavy rain and strong winds make the atmosphere outside tonight so scary.

**ghê** = scary, frightening. It describes something that makes you feel fear or shock.

It’s an adjective and usually comes **after** the noun: *phim ghê, cảnh ghê*. It can also go after verbs to comment on a situation: *đau ghê, lạnh ghê* (very painful / very cold).

In Southern speech, **ghê** often just means “very” or “intensely,” not truly scary. Close in meaning to **đáng sợ** (more formal, clearly “scary”).
